@@ -155,7 +155,7 @@ {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.1","title":"Web: Mobile Responsive Sidebar with Hamburger Menu","description":"## Problem The sidebar is fixed at 264px width and never collapses. On mobile devices (320px-768px), it consumes over 50% of screen width, making the dashboard unusable. This is a critical mobile UX failure. ## Solution Implement responsive sidebar with: 1. Hamburger menu icon on mobile (<768px) 2. Slide-out drawer pattern with overlay 3. Auto-close on navigation 4. Smooth transition animations 5. Persist collapsed state in localStorage ## Implementation Details - Add state: `const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(false)` - Add hamburger button in header for mobile - Use framer-motion for slide animation - Add backdrop with click-to-close - Media query: `md:block hidden` for desktop sidebar ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx - add collapse logic - web/src/app/layout.tsx - add mobile header with hamburger - web/src/app/globals.css - add sidebar transition styles ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Sidebar hidden by default on screens <768px - [ ] Hamburger icon visible in header on mobile - [ ] Sidebar slides in from left on tap - [ ] Backdrop overlay behind sidebar - [ ] Tap outside closes sidebar - [ ] Navigation links close sidebar - [ ] Works on 320px width screens ","notes":"## Testing Requirements ### Web E2E Tests (Playwright) ```typescript test('sidebar collapses on mobile', async ({ page }) => { await page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 667 }); await page.goto('/'); // Sidebar should be hidden await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="sidebar"]')).not.toBeVisible(); // Hamburger should be visible await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="hamburger-menu"]')).toBeVisible(); // Click hamburger opens sidebar await page.click('[data-testid="hamburger-menu"]'); await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="sidebar"]')).toBeVisible(); // Click outside closes sidebar await page.click('[data-testid="backdrop"]'); await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="sidebar"]')).not.toBeVisible(); }); ``` ### Manual Test Checklist - [ ] 320px width: sidebar hidden, hamburger visible - [ ] 768px width: sidebar always visible - [ ] Transition animation smooth - [ ] Navigation closes drawer","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:15:53.026489595-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:55.616744004-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:55.616744004-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","labels":["mobile","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.1","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:15:53.067189262-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.2","title":"Web: Add Skeleton Loading States to All Pages","description":"## Problem All pages show plain text "Loading dashboard..." which is jarring and unprofessional. Premium apps use skeleton/shimmer states that match the layout about to load. ## Solution Create skeleton components that mirror final layouts: 1. StatCardSkeleton - pulsing cards matching stat card dimensions 2. WorkerCardSkeleton - card shape with pulsing content areas 3. TableRowSkeleton - rows matching table column widths 4. Build full page skeleton compositions ## Implementation Details - Use Tailwind's `animate-pulse` for shimmer effect - Match exact dimensions of real components - Gray background bars where text will appear - Compose skeletons to match page layouts ## Component Structure ```tsx // web/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx export function Skeleton({ className }: { className?: string }) { return
} // web/src/components/stats/stat-card-skeleton.tsx export function StatCardSkeleton() { return (
) } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - web/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx - base skeleton component - web/src/components/stats/stat-card-skeleton.tsx - web/src/components/workers/worker-card-skeleton.tsx - web/src/components/builds/table-skeleton.tsx - web/src/app/page.tsx - use DashboardSkeleton - web/src/app/workers/page.tsx - use WorkersPageSkeleton - web/src/app/builds/page.tsx - use BuildsPageSkeleton ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Every page shows skeleton instead of "Loading..." - [ ] Skeletons match final layout dimensions - [ ] Smooth transition from skeleton to content - [ ] Pulse animation is subtle, not distracting ","notes":"## Testing Requirements ### Web E2E Tests (Playwright) ```typescript test('skeleton appears during loading', async ({ page }) => { // Slow down API await page.route('**/api/status', async route => { await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000)); await route.fulfill({ json: mockStatus }); }); await page.goto('/'); // Skeleton visible immediately await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="stat-card-skeleton"]')).toBeVisible(); // Wait for data await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="stat-card"]')).toBeVisible(); // Skeleton gone await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="stat-card-skeleton"]')).not.toBeVisible(); }); test('skeleton matches layout dimensions', async ({ page }) => { // Skeleton should match final layout to prevent layout shift const skeletonBox = await page.locator('[data-testid="stat-card-skeleton"]').first().boundingBox(); // Compare with real card dimensions }); ``` ### Unit Tests (React) - Skeleton renders correctly - Animation class applied","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:14.137928851-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:50:59.685620829-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:50:59.685620829-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented skeleton loading states for all pages: dashboard, workers, builds, metrics, settings. Created reusable TableSkeleton, StatCardSkeleton, WorkersGridSkeleton, and base Skeleton components.","labels":["loading","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.2","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:14.153930607-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.3","title":"Web: Enhanced Error States with Retry Buttons","description":"## Problem Error states show alert icon and message but no retry button. Users must manually refresh the entire page. This is poor UX - errors should be recoverable in-place. ## Solution Create reusable error state component with: 1. Error icon and message 2. Technical details (collapsible) 3. Retry button that re-fetches data 4. Helpful hints for common errors ## Implementation Details ```tsx // web/src/components/ui/error-state.tsx interface ErrorStateProps { error: Error | string onRetry?: () => void title?: string hint?: string } export function ErrorState({ error, onRetry, title, hint }: ErrorStateProps) { return (

{title || "Something went wrong"}

{String(error)}

{hint &&

{hint}

} {onRetry && ( )}
) } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - web/src/components/ui/error-state.tsx - new component - web/src/app/page.tsx - use ErrorState with mutate() - web/src/app/workers/page.tsx - use ErrorState - web/src/app/builds/page.tsx - use ErrorState - web/src/app/metrics/page.tsx - use ErrorState ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All pages use ErrorState component for errors - [ ] Retry button triggers data re-fetch - [ ] Helpful hints explain common issues - [ ] Error details collapsible for technical users ","notes":"## Testing Requirements ### Web E2E Tests (Playwright) ```typescript test('error state shows retry button', async ({ page }) => { // Mock API failure await page.route('**/api/status', route => route.fulfill({ status: 500 }) ); await page.goto('/'); // Error state visible await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="error-state"]')).toBeVisible(); await expect(page.getByText('Try Again')).toBeVisible(); }); test('retry button refetches data', async ({ page }) => { let callCount = 0; await page.route('**/api/status', route => { callCount++; if (callCount === 1) { route.fulfill({ status: 500 }); } else { route.fulfill({ json: mockStatus }); } }); await page.goto('/'); await page.click('text=Try Again'); // Data now visible await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="stat-card"]')).toBeVisible(); }); ``` ### Unit Tests (React Testing Library) - ErrorState renders with message - onRetry callback fires on click","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:40.153325416-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:47:22.774531335-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:47:22.774531335-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ErrorState component with retry functionality and integrated it into all 5 pages (dashboard, workers, builds, metrics, settings)","labels":["errors","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.3","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:40.181396235-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} -{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.4","title":"Web: Add Toast Notification System","description":"## Problem No user feedback for actions or state changes. When refresh completes or an action succeeds/fails, there's no indication. Premium apps provide toast notifications for user actions. ## Solution Implement toast notification system using Sonner (most popular React toast library): 1. Install sonner package 2. Add Toaster provider to layout 3. Use toast() for action feedback 4. Style toasts to match dark theme ## Usage Examples - "Data refreshed" on manual refresh - "Worker unreachable" when status changes to error - "Build completed" for active builds finishing - "Connection restored" after reconnecting ## Implementation ```tsx // web/src/app/layout.tsx import { Toaster } from 'sonner' ... // In components: import { toast } from 'sonner' toast.success('Data refreshed') toast.error('Failed to connect to daemon') ``` ## Files to Modify - package.json - add sonner dependency - web/src/app/layout.tsx - add Toaster - web/src/app/page.tsx - add refresh toast - web/src/app/workers/page.tsx - add action toasts ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Toast appears on manual refresh - [ ] Toast shows on error recovery - [ ] Toasts auto-dismiss after 4 seconds - [ ] Toasts match dark theme styling - [ ] Can dismiss toasts manually ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.512545526-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:22:45.146811025-05:00","labels":["feedback","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.4","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.583372542-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} +{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.4","title":"Web: Add Toast Notification System","description":"## Problem No user feedback for actions or state changes. When refresh completes or an action succeeds/fails, there's no indication. Premium apps provide toast notifications for user actions. ## Solution Implement toast notification system using Sonner (most popular React toast library): 1. Install sonner package 2. Add Toaster provider to layout 3. Use toast() for action feedback 4. Style toasts to match dark theme ## Usage Examples - "Data refreshed" on manual refresh - "Worker unreachable" when status changes to error - "Build completed" for active builds finishing - "Connection restored" after reconnecting ## Implementation ```tsx // web/src/app/layout.tsx import { Toaster } from 'sonner' ... // In components: import { toast } from 'sonner' toast.success('Data refreshed') toast.error('Failed to connect to daemon') ``` ## Files to Modify - package.json - add sonner dependency - web/src/app/layout.tsx - add Toaster - web/src/app/page.tsx - add refresh toast - web/src/app/workers/page.tsx - add action toasts ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Toast appears on manual refresh - [ ] Toast shows on error recovery - [ ] Toasts auto-dismiss after 4 seconds - [ ] Toasts match dark theme styling - [ ] Can dismiss toasts manually ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.512545526-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:33:34.812311364-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T19:33:34.812311364-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: Sonner provider + dashboard/workers toasts + 4s duration","labels":["feedback","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.4","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:10.583372542-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.5","title":"Web: Redesign Metrics Page for Non-DevOps Users","description":"## Problem Metrics page shows raw Prometheus text format in a pre tag. This is unhelpful for users who aren't DevOps engineers. Metrics should be visualized and explained. ## Current State - Raw text dump of Prometheus metrics - No explanation of what metrics mean - No visualization of trends - Budget section is good but sparse ## Solution Redesign metrics page with: 1. Key metrics as visual cards (similar to stat cards) 2. Mini sparklines for trends (if historical data available) 3. Expandable "raw metrics" section for power users 4. Metric explanations/tooltips ## Metrics to Highlight - Total compilations (counter) - Success rate (percentage gauge) - Average compilation time (with trend) - Active workers (current count) - Circuit breaker status (visual indicator) - Network transfer volume ## Implementation ```tsx
Raw Prometheus Metrics 
{metricsText}
``` ## Files to Modify - web/src/app/metrics/page.tsx - complete redesign - web/src/components/metrics/metric-card.tsx - new component - web/src/lib/api.ts - parse metrics into structured data ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Key metrics displayed as visual cards - [ ] Each metric has explanation tooltip - [ ] Raw metrics available but collapsed by default - [ ] Page is useful for non-technical users ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.276259325-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.276259325-05:00","labels":["metrics","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.5","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:39.471211673-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.6","title":"Web: Add Sorting and Filtering to Tables","description":"## Problem Build history and worker tables have no sorting or filtering. With many builds or workers, users can't find what they need quickly. Tables should be interactive. ## Solution Add to tables: 1. Column headers clickable for sorting (asc/desc) 2. Sort indicator icons 3. Filter input for searching 4. Pagination for large datasets ## Implementation Details - Use React state for sort column and direction - Client-side sorting (data already loaded) - Filter with debounced input - Visual sort indicators (▲/▼) ## Build History Table Filters - Status filter (success/failed/all) - Worker filter (dropdown) - Date range filter - Command search ## Workers Table Sorting - Sort by status (healthy first) - Sort by slot availability - Sort by speed score ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/builds/build-history-table.tsx - add sorting/filtering - web/src/components/workers/workers-grid.tsx - add sorting - web/src/components/ui/table.tsx - add sortable header component ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Click column header to sort - [ ] Sort direction indicator visible - [ ] Filter input filters table rows - [ ] Filters persist during polling updates - [ ] Pagination for >50 rows ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.799142181-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:30:34.046792095-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T19:30:34.046792095-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented sorting, filtering, and pagination controls","labels":["tables","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.6","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.800559508-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","title":"Web: WCAG AA Accessibility Compliance","description":"## Problem Dashboard has accessibility gaps: - No aria-labels on custom components - Keyboard navigation not fully tested - Focus indicators could be stronger - Truncated content only accessible via hover ## Solution Audit and fix accessibility issues: 1. Add aria-labels to all interactive elements 2. Ensure all actions keyboard-accessible 3. Add visible focus rings 4. Make truncated content expandable 5. Add skip-to-main link ## Specific Fixes Needed - Sidebar nav: aria-current for active link - Refresh buttons: aria-label="Refresh data" - Progress bars: aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax - Tables: scope="col" on headers, aria-sort - Truncated commands: expandable on focus, not just hover - Color contrast: verify all text meets 4.5:1 ratio ## Testing - Tab through entire app - Test with VoiceOver/NVDA - Run Lighthouse accessibility audit - Use axe browser extension ## Files to Modify - web/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx - aria-current - web/src/components/ui/button.tsx - aria-label support - web/src/components/ui/progress.tsx - aria attributes - web/src/components/ui/table.tsx - scope, aria-sort - web/src/components/builds/build-history-table.tsx - expandable commands ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Lighthouse accessibility score > 90 - [ ] All interactive elements keyboard accessible - [ ] Screen reader announces all content - [ ] Focus visible on all interactive elements - [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1) ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.162061803-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:19:23.229486336-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:19:23.229486336-05:00","close_reason":"Added skip link, aria labels, progress/table accessibility, nav focus/aria-current","labels":["a11y","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:08.163335169-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.248582142-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:46.517116699-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.7","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ngl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:47.212590316-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
